item.title: Memory Manager
item.description:
Link: https://github.com/css186/External-Sorting

Project Description Link to heading

This project is a simulation program for storing, managing records and handling memory allocation and data retrieval using a combination of a hash table and a memory manager.

Here is a breakdown of its components and functionality:

Core Functionality Link to heading

  1. Hash Table (HashTable.java)

    • Uses quadratic probing for collision resolution.
    • Stores Record objects that pair seminar IDs with memory handles.
    • Automatically resizes when the load factor exceeds a threshold.

  2. Memory Manager(MemManager.java)

item.title: External Sorting
item.description:
Link: https://github.com/css186/External-Sorting

Project Description Link to heading

This program implements an external sorting algorithm designed to handle large datasets that exceed memory capacity. It uses replacement selection to create sorted runs and multiway merging to combine them into a single sorted output.

Here is a breakdown of its components and functionality:

Core Functionality Link to heading

  1. External Sorting

    • Replacement Selection (ReplacementSelection.java)

item.title: Tree-Based Seminar Database
item.description:
Link: https://github.com/css186/Tree-Based-Seminar-Database

Project Description Link to heading

This project is a seminar database that supports multi-criteria storage, retrieval, and spatial queries. It organizes seminars’ information using binary search trees (BSTs) for attribute-based indexing and a spatial tree called “Bintree” for location-based queries..

All data structures in this project were built from scratch. The only built-in container allowed is the static array

Here is a breakdown of its components and functionality:

item.title: Graph and Hash Table Based Song/Artist Database
item.description:
Link: https://github.com/css186/Graph-and-Hash-Table-Based-Song-Artist-Database

Project Description Link to heading

This project is a simulated database for storing songs, artists information, and tracking relationships between them. The records stored inside are artist names and track names. The two main data structures used are a hash table and a graph.

All data structures in this project were built from scratch. The only built-in container allowed is the static array

Here is a breakdown of its components and functionality:

item.title: About
item.description:
Intro Link to heading

Hello there! I’m Brian, a graduate student in Computer Science from Virginia Tech.

I have versatile experience in project management, data analytics/machine learning, financial technology, and software development, and I am also passionate about creating innovative solutions.

Check out my resume!

Technical Skills Link to heading

  • Languages: Python, Java, C++/C
  • Web Development: HTML5, CSS3, Bootstrap, Flask, FastAPI, SpringBoot
  • Databases: PostgreSQL, MySQL, SQL Server, MongoDB
  • Tools: Git, Docker, AWS, Visual Studio Code
  • Data: NumPy, pandas, matplotlib, seaborn, scikit-learn, PyTorch

Goal Link to heading

My goal is to specialize further in backend development. I am currently seeking internship opportunities in software engineering where I can apply my skills in a collaborative environment and contribute to meaningful projects.
